pipeline_design.overview: Three-stage pipeline: (1) classify input mode, (2) extract/normalize content, (3) generate summary and build canonical output

stage_1_classify.description: Determines how to process the input based on content type and source

modes.article: Full text preservation with cleaning. Sources: news, blogs, newsletters, essays
modes.link: Metadata + short 'about' text only. Sources: tools, products, apps, generic links
modes.profile: Structured metadata extraction. Sources: LinkedIn, personal sites, company pages
modes.resource: Metadata + description. Sources: GitHub repos, documentation, reference material
modes.social: Preserve post content verbatim. Sources: X/Twitter, LinkedIn posts
modes.reference: Citation metadata only. Sources: papers, books (metadata-only entries)

classification_signals: URL domain patterns (substack, medium → article; github.com → resource; x.com, twitter.com → social)
Structural patterns (h1-only + paragraphs = article; lists/tables = resource)

stage_2_extract.function: extract_clean_content(filepath: Path, html_path: Path | None, mode: IngestMode) -> ExtractedContent
stage_2_extract.description: Extracts clean content using mode-appropriate strategy

html_preferred.fallback: Original markdown if trafilatura returns empty/too-short

markdown_fallback.when: No HTML available or trafilatura fails
patterns_to_remove: Sign in / Sign up / Log in
Footer sections (© YYYY, Terms, Privacy, Cookie)

social_passthrough.method: Preserve original markdown, strip only obvious nav/footer
social_passthrough.rationale: Social posts are short; full content IS the value

output.title: string - extracted or generated from filename
output.author: string | null

article_mode.prompt_template: Summarize this article in 1-3 sentences. Focus on the main thesis or key takeaway. Article: {clean_text[:4000]}
article_mode.fallback: First 200 chars of clean_text if LLM unavailable

link_mode.method: Use meta description if available, else LLM
link_mode.output: Brief 'about' text describing what the resource is

social_mode.method: No summary needed - content IS the summary

stage_3_summarize.cost_awareness: LLM calls are expensive; cache summaries, skip for short content (<500 chars)

stage_4_output.function: build_canonical_output(extracted: ExtractedContent, summary: str, mode: IngestMode) -> str
stage_4_output.description: Assembles final markdown with enriched frontmatter

mode_specific.link: Include only 'about' text (50-200 chars), not full scrape

trafilatura.use_for: HTML → clean text extraction, metadata extraction

recommended_libraries.recommendation: trafilatura alone handles 90%+ of cases. Add readability-lxml only if specific failures emerge.

article_signals: Multiple h2/h3 headings with substantial paragraphs between

assertions: assert 'Skip to content' not in output
assert len(output) < len(input) * 0.5 for article/resource modes
assert 'summary' in frontmatter for article mode

modify_existing: ingest_file() - add normalize=True flag (default True for new ingests)

flag_strategy.rationale: New ingests get normalization by default; --no-normalize for raw preservation

implementation_notes.backward_compatibility: Existing files in content-library unchanged unless re-ingested with --normalize

notes_for_orchestrator: TRAFILATURA WORKS EXCELLENTLY - reduced 274KB GitHub HTML to 3.6KB clean content. Should be primary extraction method.
X/TWITTER FALLBACK NEEDED - trafilatura fails on JS-rendered content. For social posts, use original markdown with light boilerplate stripping.
SUMMARY GENERATION COST - LLM summaries add latency/cost. Recommend: (1) skip for content <500 chars, (2) use meta description when available, (3) cache summaries.
CLASSIFICATION CONFIDENCE - URL domain matching handles 80% of cases. Content heuristics needed for edge cases.
HTML COMPANION FILES - save_webpage saves both .md and .html. The ingest script should look for companion HTML files to enable trafilatura extraction.
NO IMPLEMENTATION YET - This is design-only per brief. Ready for Builder to implement in W2 wave.
CONSIDER: Adding a 'normalized_at' timestamp to frontmatter so we can track which files have been processed.
DEPENDENCY: W1.2 (schema) should include 'summary' field in items table.
